mission.title: Install and Configure NATS
mission.description: NATS is a high-performance messaging system designed for cloud-native applications and microservices. It provides a lightweight and scalable solution for communication between services, making it ideal for distributed systems. Key trade-offs include simplicity and performance versus advanced messaging features found in other systems.

steps.title: Add the NATS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official NATS Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add nats https://nats-io.github.io/nats-helm

```

> This repository contains the NATS Helm charts.

steps.title: Install NATS with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs NATS in the 'nats' namespace using the specified version of the Helm chart.

```bash

kubectl create namespace nats

helm install nats nats/nats --namespace nats --version 0.14.0

```

> Create a namespace for NATS.
> Install NATS using the specified chart version.

steps.title: Verify NATS installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the NATS pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> List all pods in the NATS namespace.

steps.title: Create a sample NATS configuration
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource (CR) for NATS to demonstrate its usage.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

```

> Deploy a NATS cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify NATS custom resource
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the NATS custom resource to ensure it is ready.

```bash

kubectl get nats -n nats

```

> List all NATS custom resources in the namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall NATS
uninstall.description:
This step removes the NATS installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall nats --namespace nats

kubectl delete namespace nats

```

> Uninstall the NATS release.
> Delete the NATS namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade NATS
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configuration and data. This step will upgrade NATS to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get nats -n nats -o yaml > nats-backup.yaml

helm upgrade nats nats/nats --namespace nats --version 0.14.1

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> Backup the current NATS configuration.
> Upgrade NATS to the specified version.
> Verify that the NATS pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get nats -n nats -o yaml > nats-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: NATS pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the NATS server settings.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the NATS pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n nats' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to NATS server
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or firewall rules may be blocking access to the NATS service.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the network policies allow traffic to the NATS service and check service endpoints.

troubleshooting.title: NATS custom resource not ready
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The NATS operator may not be able to create the necessary resources due to insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Verify that the operator has the correct RBAC permissions to manage resources in the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Helm chart version may not exist in the repository.

**Fix:**
Check the available chart versions with 'helm search repo nats' and use a valid version.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for NATS JetStream, improved monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.10, review the JetStream documentation for configuration changes.

resolution.summary: You should see the NATS pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n nats'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
